title: Add copy-to-clipboard button for generator code
author: CodeKageFX
body:
### Problem
Currently, users have to manually select and copy the generated generator code, which is slightly inconvenient, especially for long outputs.

### Proposed Solution
Add a copy-to-clipboard button near the generator code so users can quickly copy the content with one click.

### Benefits
- Improves user experience
- Saves time for users
- Makes the tool more user-friendly

If this sounds good, I’d love to work on this and submit a PR.

title: Feature: Complete VSCode Extension Implementation
author: samueltuoyo15
body:
### Feature Request

I've started the scaffolding for the Dokugen VSCode extension in `apps/vscode-extension`, but it still needs core functionality to be fully usable. I'm looking for help to bring this to life!

### Goal
Build a working VSCode extension that allows users to run Dokugen directly from their editor command palette or right-click context menu.

###  Tasks Checklist
- [ ] **Command Implementation**: ensuring `Dokugen: Generate Readme` triggers the generation flow.
- [ ] **Interactive UI**: Use VSCode input boxes / quick picks similar to the CLI prompts (Project Type, Setup Instructions, etc.).
- [ ] **Output View**: Stream the generation logs/progress to a VSCode output channel instead of the terminal.
- [ ] **File Integration**: Ensure the generated `README.md` is created in the active workspace root and opened upon completion.

### Current State
- The folder structure exists at `apps/vscode-extension`.
- Basic `package.json` and config might be there, but the main logic inside `src/` needs work.

### Tech Stack
- TypeScript
- VSCode Extension API

###  Contribution
If you're interested in taking this up, please comment below! Check `CONTRIBUTION.md` for guidelines on setting up the repo locally.

title: Feature: Add dokugen commit subcommand for AI-assisted commit message generation
author: Petsamuel
body:
Problem / Use Case
A lot of devs still struggle with writing clean, consistent commit messages. Imagine a generator that reads your git diff --cached, auto-suggests commit types (feat, fix, chore, etc.), and even spins up an AI-assisted summary that actually sounds human kinda like how we auto-generate READMEs.

Proposed Solution
Add a new command with simple flags say --template for Conventional Commits or --ai for smart descriptions. If we’ve already got AI hooks in Dokugen, it should plug right in.

Potential Impact
This would expand Dokugen’s automation game in a real way devs love anything that removes friction from their workflow. It’d pull in more users and make Dokugen feel smarter and more intuitive.

title: Feature Request: Generate documentation for an entire project.
author: struckchure
body:
### Description
Extending the CLI to generate documentation for core code components (or interactively ask which parts to document)

### Use Case
Project owners can generate documentation for core parts of their application, and even document each file.

title: docs: improvements and corrections in README.md
author: TopeAanu
body:
### Description
This issue addresses several grammar errors and formatting inconsistencies

### Proposed Changes

#### Grammar and Style Corrections
1. Added missing articles throughout the document (e.g., "a lightweight" instead of "lightweight")
2. Fixed verb forms (e.g., "generating" instead of "generate")
3. Corrected capitalization of language names (PHP, TypeScript, Express.js)
4. Fixed prepositions and conjunctions for clarity
5. Improved sentence structure for readability
6. Corrected "Programming Language" spelling (was "Progrmamming")

#### Content Improvements
1. Added an "Installation" section with both npm and yarn installation methods
2. Added the author's GitHub profile link, which was missing
3. Improved section headers for clarity
4. Clarified "Programming Language and Framework Agnostic" feature description
5. Changed "Author Info" to simply "Author" for consistency with other headings

#### Formatting Enhancements
1. Fixed inconsistent spacing in code blocks
2. Standardized capitalization in headings
3. Improved readability of list items
4. Made punctuation consistent 
5. Ensured proper spacing between sections

### Impact
With these changes, the readability of the README.md will be clearer.

title: User Selection Bug
author: codesignerd
body: Dokugen is really a great package and it helps saves lot of time with writing read.me files as promised.  But I noticed this package would stil go ahead and include a setup instructions and contribution instructions even after selecting no. KIndly look into this to give us  a more better user experience.
